title: Nursery Rhyme. XVII. Historical
content:
Over the water, and over the lee,
        And over the water to Charley.
        Charley loves good ale and wine,
        And Charley loves good brandy,
        And Charley loves a pretty girl,
        As sweet as sugar-candy.

        Over the water, and over the sea,
        And over the water to Charley,
        I'll have none of your nasty beef,
        Nor I'll have none of your barley;
        But I'll have some of your very best flour;
        To make a white cake for my Charley.
meaning:
Unknown's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Nursery Rhyme. XVII. Historical"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
